rulings.Case Title: LARRY DUBAN VS. DANIEL BERKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling The San Francisco Superior Court To Pay, From Funds Held By The Court, Monies Necessary To Pay The Judgment Entered In Fthis Action Agaisnt Larry Duban And In Favor Of Judgment Creditor Daniel Berko And The Two Sanctions Awards Made In This Case; Declaration; Requesst For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 21, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO Motion For an Order Compelling The San Francisco Superior Court To Pay, From Funds Held By The Court, Monies Necessary To Pay The Judgment Entered In this Action Against Larry Duban and In Favor of Judgment Creditor Daniel Berko and The Two Sanctions Awards Made In This Case. Motion continued to January 26, 2010. Mr. Duban to post a bond in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 917.1 by January 19, 2010 to prevent release of Berko's share of the funds held in interpleader. If Mr. Duban does not post a bond, the Court will release the requested funds to Mr. Berko. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RESTAURANTS INTERNATIONAL, INC. A CORPORATION et al VS. NANCY MAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 21, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANTS NANCY MAR, 1660 SACRAMENTO STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied for first, second, and third causes of action, failure to shift the burden of proof. The applicable statutes of limitation for the first and second causes of action were not raised as affirmative defenses and are waived. Motion denied as to third cause of action because motion denied as to second cause of action. Motion granted as to fourth cause of action. Claim is barred by Anderson vs. Anderson (1995) 41 Cal.App.4th 135 and Probate Code section 9353. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PIRUZ VARGA VS. REGENTS UNIV., OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 21, 2009, line 14, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM LEW, ROBERT EATON, KEITH CUDABACK, and, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend as to The Regents. Plaintiff cannot obtain punitives against The Regents, as a matter of law. See Gov't Code section 818. Granted with leave to amend as to individual defendants. =(302/CWW)
